summary: Independently verify rebalanced §3/§4 (4 lenses) and author+verify §7, into scratch drafts

s7.summary:
Authored PRD §7 (Milestone Roadmap, v1 Scope & Deployment) as a DRAFT into /Users/raynos/projects/games/incremental-game-1/docs/prd-sections/section-7-roadmap.md, in the house style of §3/§4 (DRAFT-awaiting-review banner, macron romanization, lean/high-impact, by-number cross-refs, a "flagged for the human" subsection). §7.1 fixes the definitive v1 scope (T0–T2 complete incl. the T2 personal-mystery payoff; T3 = stub cliff-hanger; T4 = roadmap), the lean cut-set (~8-rung ladders ×3 = 24 rungs, ~5 mobs, 4 quest types, ~6–8-node maps, the locked skill set, E0–E2 stages), the LOCKED pacing target (≈28.5 h: T0 ≈ 4.5 h / T1 ≈ 8 h / T2 ≈ 16 h, ≥30-min/rank floor) as a CI acceptance criterion, and an explicit PARKED/CUT list (E3–E5, Matagi, Pilgrimage Order, Scholars network, auto-producers, marriage/adoption, deeper upper-tier nodes, the banzuke, no-respec). §7.2 lays out M0…M7 as verifiable vertical slices (M0 toolchain+cold-open+save; M1 T0 labour R0→R2; M2 combat live at R3; M3 quests+crafting+4-pillar panel → T0 done; M4 T1 Village V0→V7; M5 T2 Region G0→G7; M6 balance pass+verifier-green+T3 stub+polish; M7 deploy), each with goal, §-systems landed, and a verify-green definition-of-done, plus a summary table. §7.3 covers the static itch.io build (relative base path, single HTML bundle), the export/import save path, the npm run verify release gate, and a brief how-to-ship. §7.4 gives a 5-risk register (T2 scope, balance-tuning time, save migration, art/feel, the dense combat slice) and a cut-down ladder whose minimum-shippable fallback is T0–T1 only. Every canon constraint is honoured (pure-core/deterministic/single-RNG, active-only / no idle layer, no people-management sim, combat-earns-Arms, no magic, K/M/B, macrons); assumptions are tagged "proposed — for review." Verified macron correctness (Tōge-mura etc.) and no plain-ASCII slips.</summary>
<parameter name="openQuestions">["Milestone granularity: should the dense combat slice M2 be split into fixed up-front milestones M2a (idle auto-resolve + first fight) / M2b (bestiary/equipment/loot-craft), or left as the agent's build-time call at the R3→R4 seam?", "Cut-down floor: is T0–T1-only the right minimum-shippable line (complete two-tier arc ending on the T1→T2 cliff-hanger, preserving combat/4-pillars/crafting/rep-web), with T0-only as a demo-grade floor used only in dire circumstances?", "itch.io page specifics: confirm free/pay-what-you-want, Kind=HTML play-in-browser, relative base ('./'), and a responsive default frame — any fixed canvas size or embed-width constraint to pin before M7?", "CI host: should npm run verify + the §4.8 pacing regression run in hosted CI (e.g. GitHub Actions) on every push, or is running verify locally as the pre-push gate sufficient for the solo/agentic workflow?", "Release process: confirm the itch.io upload stays a MANUAL, human-approved step per release (no itch CLI / butler automation in v1)?", "ADR numbering: §7 proposes recording the v1 scope + M0…M7 build order + deployment/verify-gate + cut-down ladder as 'D-016 — v1 execution plan' (an execution checkpoint, not a design reversal) — confirm the number/framing at integration."]

pacing.summary: The §4 pacing math is overwhelmingly sound and internally arithmetic-consistent. I independently reproduced every load-bearing number: the T0 table sums to 270 min = 4.5 h (all 7 grind rungs >=30 min and escalating, R0 the ~5-min exempt cold open); each T0 grind rung's (throughput x time) reproduces the stated lifetime-koku figures EXACTLY (750/1050/1200/2100/3200/4950/8250 -> 21.5K); E1@R4 (cum 5.1K) and E2@R6 (cum 13.25K) affordability ties out; T1=480 min=8 h, T2=960 min=16 h, cumulative 4.5/12.5/28.5 h all consistent; per-event caps (0.04 x band-top) all match; tier-gate thresholds sit inside their §4.0 bands; tier steps (10x/10x, 6x/7.5x/25x) match and the 25x Office step is explicitly flagged as Open Question #5; season bindings give ~8 seasons/tier; and the 28.5 h built + 3.5 h T3-stub-tail = 32 h reconciliation is coherent and honestly labeled. HOWEVER I found three concrete inconsistencies, one of them material: (1) the §4.2.1 claim that the per-event cap 'rarely binds' is FALSE for Office in T1 and several deeds in T2 — the cap silently clamps stated deed bases (T1 Office 130->80, 240->80; T2 road-safe 1500->1200, defend 2000->1200, valley-allied 2400->2000), which means the doc's own deed-count arithmetic for the Office gate uses uncapped bases it then forbids; (2) the §4.8.1 totals line states a combined ~56-60 deeds across T0 but quotes the per-pillar cadence of '~9-10 min/deed' — combined it is ~4.7 min/deed; (3) the §4.2.1 itemized Estate deed breakdown only delivers 486 ip toward a 560-ip (70%) target, needing ~5 unspecified 'filler' deeds that push the count past the stated '~28-30'. None of these break the locked time budget or the tier-up gates' reachability; they are accounting/narrative inconsistencies in the accrual section.

findings.location: section-4-balance.md §4.2.1 (lines ~158-160, deed-base table lines 163-174) cross-checked against §4.0 bands and §4.2.1 cap formula
findings.issue: The claim 'Most deed bases below sit at or under that cap, so the cap rarely binds' is contradicted by the doc's own numbers. T1 Office cap = 0.04 x 2K band-top = 80 ip, but the two T1 Office deed bases are 130 ('Office granted/bailiff/arbitrated') and 240 ('Valley allied/rival eclipsed') — both EXCEED the cap, so the alliance deed is clamped to 1/3 of its stated base. In T2 the cap also binds on 'Road/ford safe' Arms (base 1500 vs cap 0.04x30K=1200), 'Grain-store/valley defended' Arms (2000 vs 1200), and 'Valley allied' Office (2400 vs cap 0.04x50K=2000). Because the T1 Office gate (2K ip, the hardest T1 gate) is the one most throttled, the §4.8.2 deed-count math (which implicitly uses uncapped bases ~185 avg -> ~7.5 deeds) understates the real requirement (~17.5 capped Office deeds at 80 ip each). The gate is still reachable, but the 'cap rarely binds' narrative and the implied deed counts are wrong precisely where it matters most.
findings.suggestedFix: Either (a) lower the listed T1/T2 Office and the T2 road/defend deed bases to sit at-or-under their 0.04 x band-top caps (e.g. T1 Office deeds <=80; T2 valley-allied <=2000, road-safe/defend <=1200), or (b) keep the higher bases but rewrite the 'cap rarely binds' sentence to say the cap DOES bind on Office (and high-end T2 Arms) deeds — and redo the affected deed-count tie-outs in §4.8.2/§4.8.3 using the clamped 80-ip (and 1200/2000-ip) values so the Office-gate deed count is stated honestly.

findings.issue: The totals line states 'Deeds across T0 ~ 28-30 Estate + 28-30 Arms, one recognised act every ~9-10 min'. The ~9-10 min cadence is correct for ONE pillar in isolation (§4.2.1: 270 min / 29 deeds = 9.3 min), but here both pillars' deeds are summed (~56-60 total deeds over 270 min), which is one recognised act every ~4.7 min, not ~9-10 min. The cadence figure was carried over from the per-pillar derivation while citing the combined count.
findings.suggestedFix: Change to '...~56-60 total recognised deeds, one act every ~4.5-5 min (or ~9-10 min per individual pillar)' so the combined deed count and the cadence are arithmetically consistent.

findings.issue: The itemized T0 Estate breakdown sums to 486 ip (1x30 + 12x16 + 6x24 + 10x12), short of the stated 70%-of-0.8K = 560 ip target by 74 ip (~13%). Reaching 560 needs ~5 more 'filler' deeds (~16 ip each), pushing the Estate deed count to ~34 — above the stated '~28-30 recognised deeds'. So the deed-count claim and the ip-target claim cannot both hold with the given bases (the 'filler' silently does ~13% of the work). Arms is self-consistent: itemized 304 ip / 23 deeds + ~6 filler deeds at 8 ip reaches both ~350 ip and ~29 deeds.
findings.suggestedFix: Bump one or two Estate deed bases (e.g. yield milestone 16->18, solvency 24->26) or add the filler deeds explicitly to the itemization so the breakdown lands on ~560 ip within the stated ~28-30 count, rather than relying on unstated filler to close a 13% gap.

findings.issue: Canon §I-bal asserts 'With 8 rungs, this [>=30 min floor] makes T0 >= 4h BY CONSTRUCTION' (8 x 30 = 240 min). The §4 draft, however, treats R0 as a sub-30-min cold-open EXEMPT from the floor (~5 min), leaving only 7 floored grind rungs (7 x 30 = 210 min + R0). The doc still reaches 4.5h, but it does so by escalating later rungs (35/40/45/55), NOT by the canon's '8 rungs at the floor' construction. The literal 'by construction' arithmetic in canon does not hold once one rung is exempt. This is defensible (4.5h > 4h target) and the doc is transparent about R0, but the divergence from canon's stated reasoning is worth a one-line note.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a sentence in §4.8.1 noting that the >=30-min floor applies to the 7 grind rungs (R1-R7); R0 is the exempt cold-open, so T0's 4.5h comes from floor+escalation rather than canon's literal '8 rungs x 30 min = 4h by construction' phrasing.

findings.location: section-4-balance.md §4.0 T0 band row (line 61) vs §4.8.1 totals (line 624)
findings.issue: The §4.0 magnitude band states T0 'tens -> ~20K produced', while the §4.8.1 throughput sum is 21.5K. A ~7.5% gap. Both are explicitly approximate ('~20K', '~21.5K'), so this is tilde-level, but the master-scale table and the centrepiece pacing table cite slightly different round numbers for the same quantity.
findings.suggestedFix: Align the §4.0 T0 band to '~21K produced' (or change the §4.8.1 sum's headline to '~20K') so the two tables quote the same T0 lifetime-koku figure.

economy.summary:
Adversarial verification of the §4 economy/accrual model against the §I-bal balance locks. The HARD INVARIANTS hold: the F1 trade-cap fix is algebraically exact (trade/(land+treasury+trade) = 1/3 precisely at the cap, < 1/3 below it — verified numerically), dents are up-only/recoverable/never-a-wipe and never touch highWater, and there is no labour→combat cross-feed anywhere (attributes do double-duty, which is canon-allowed; labour SKILLS never feed combat stats). The deed-jump cap was correctly halved to 0.04 and deed bases re-tuned downward with consistent ~9–10× per-tier scaling.

However, the ACCRUAL SPLIT (check 1) fails verification on two independent counts. FIRST: the doc's own itemized §4.2.1 deed inventory does not sum to the claimed 70%. T0 Estate deeds total 1*30 + 12*16 + 6*24 + 10*12 = 486 ip = 60.8% of the 800 ip gate (not 70%), and T0 Arms deeds total 6*18 + 5*20 + 12*8 = 304 ip = 60.8% of 500 (not 70%). 60.8% deeds / 39.2% seasonal is OUTSIDE the human-locked ⅔–¾ deeds / ¼–⅓ seasonal band (deeds below the 66.7% floor; seasonal above the 33.3% ceiling). The §4.8 R7 totals line CLAIMS "560 ip from ~28 deeds" / "350 deed-ip" but the §4.2.1 table it cites yields 486/304 — an internal contradiction. SECOND, and more serious: the JUDGE_K values cannot produce the claimed ~30% seasonal share because the f_pillar inputs are dimensionally uncontrolled. Estate's basis is sqrt(landReclaimedKoku); with JUDGE_K=55 and realistic end-T0 land koku in the hundreds-to-thousands, the seasonal score is 55*sqrt(500..3000) ≈ 1200–3000 ip, which ALONE blows past the entire 800 ip gate by 1.5–4×. To hit the claimed 240 ip the landReclaimedKoku input would have to be ~19 koku — impossible against ~21K lifetime-produced. This is the SAME un-normalized-magic-scale bug class the F4 fix caught for Name (the bare /1000), but it is left UNFIXED for Estate, Arms, and Office. The four JUDGE_K (45/55/38/34) are applied to bases with wildly inconsistent units (Estate sqrt-of-thousands ≈ O(50–70); Arms/Office small integers ≈ O(2–4); Name ≈ O(0.3)), so they are not co-derived to any consistent target and the "JUDGE_K actually produce the split" claim is unverifiable as written.

Check 4 (F4): the /1000 is now the named JUDGE_NAME_BLEND, but folding 0.25/1000 into f_name while still multiplying by JUDGE_K[Name]=34 means the effective coefficient is 34*0.00025, not 0.00025 — so this is a 34× behavioral change dressed as a pure rename. Either keep 0.25 outside JUDGE_K or document that the rename changed the magnitude.

Net: the locked SHAPES are honored (trade cap, dents, no cross-feed, deeds-dominate direction, smaller cap), but the proposed NUMBERS do not actually land the locked 70/30 deeds/seasonal split, and the seasonal formula is mis-scaled/unverifiable due to undefined f_pillar units. These are tunable (proposed) numbers, so this is fixable without architecture changes — hence PASS_WITH_FIXES — but the §4.8 tie-out tables currently assert checkmarks (✔) that the §4.2.1/§4.2.2 numbers do not support.

findings.location: docs/prd-sections/section-4-balance.md §4.2.1 deed table + §4.8.1 R7 totals line (lines 163-182, 624-629)
findings.issue: The itemized T0 deed inventory does NOT sum to the locked ⅔–¾ deeds band, contradicting the doc's own 70/30 claim. T0 Estate listed deeds = 1*30 + 12*16 + 6*24 + 10*12 = 486 ip = 60.8% of the 800 ip gate (the §4.8 totals line claims 560 ip / 70%). T0 Arms listed deeds = 6*18 + 5*20 + 12*8 = 304 ip = 60.8% of the 500 ip gate (claims 350 ip / 70%). 60.8% deeds / 39.2% seasonal is OUTSIDE the human-locked 66.7–75% deeds / 25–33.3% seasonal band — deeds fall below the floor, seasonal exceeds the ceiling. The §4.8 ✔ checkmarks assert a split the §4.2.1 table cannot produce.
findings.suggestedFix: Re-tune the deed inventory so deed-ip = 70% of each gate: Estate needs +74 ip (e.g. raise yield-milestone count to ~14 and/or solvency to ~7, or bump a base), Arms needs +46 ip (e.g. +2 road-clears or +3 minor-clears). Then re-derive seasonal to the residual 30% and update the §4.8.1 totals line so 486→560 / 304→350 reconcile. Verify the recomputed split lands in 66.7–75%, not just at 70%.

findings.location: docs/prd-sections/section-4-balance.md §4.2.2 seasonal formula + JUDGE_K table (lines 196-235); §4.8.1 claim (lines 624-629)
findings.issue: The JUDGE_K values cannot produce the claimed ~30% seasonal share because f_pillar inputs are dimensionally uncontrolled and undefined. Estate basis = sqrt(landReclaimedKoku) with JUDGE_K=55: at realistic end-T0 land koku (hundreds-to-thousands) the seasonal high-water score = 55*sqrt(500..3000) ≈ 1200–3000 ip, ALONE 1.5–4× the entire 800 ip gate. Hitting the claimed 240 ip would require landReclaimedKoku ≈ 19 koku — impossible against ~21K lifetime-produced. Worse, the four JUDGE_K (45/55/38/34) multiply bases of incompatible scale: Estate sqrt(thousands) ≈ O(50–70), Arms/Office small integers ≈ O(2–4), Name ≈ O(0.3) — so Estate's seasonal would dwarf the others by ~40×. The JUDGE_K are not co-derived to any consistent target; the 'JUDGE_K actually produce the 30% split' claim is unverifiable/false as written. This is the SAME un-normalized magic-scale bug the F4 fix caught for Name (/1000), left UNFIXED for Estate/Arms/Office.
findings.suggestedFix: Define explicit units/normalization for every f_pillar input (e.g. landReclaimedKoku normalized by a named TIER_KOKU_REF[tier] so the sqrt term is O(1) at band-top), then back-solve each JUDGE_K so its pillar's cumulative high-water gain over the tier's ~8 seasons equals exactly 30% of that pillar's gate (Estate 240, Arms 150). Add a worked numeric tie-out (start/end accumulatedState values) the way §4.8.1 does for koku, so JUDGE_K is verifiable. Apply the F4 normalization treatment uniformly to all four pillars, not just Name.

findings.issue: The F4 fix names the lever but silently changes magnitude. The old basis was the bare expression 0.25*(armsHW+estateHW+officeHW)/1000 (the whole f, no JUDGE_K). The new form sets f_name = JUDGE_NAME_BLEND*(sumHW)+patronage with JUDGE_NAME_BLEND=0.00025, AND multiplies by JUDGE_K[Name]=34. Effective coefficient is now 34*0.00025 = 0.0085 — a 34× increase, not a pure rename. The note claims 'no hardcoded constants' and 'remains derivative' but does not flag that folding 0.25/1000 into f while keeping JUDGE_K=34 double-scales.
findings.suggestedFix: Either (a) pull the 0.25 OUT of JUDGE_NAME_BLEND and treat JUDGE_K[Name] as the only outer scaler (set JUDGE_K[Name] to absorb the intended /1000), or (b) explicitly state the F4 rename also re-magnitudes Name by 34× and confirm that is intended. Document the resulting effective Name coefficient (34*0.00025=0.0085) in the §4.9 index.

findings.issue: Two deed bases are labeled '(cap)' but are not actually at the cap. T0 Estate cap = 0.04*800 = 32 ip, yet 'First shinden plot' base = 30 is labeled '30 (cap)' (30 < 32, not capped). T0 Arms 'Grain-store defended' base = 20 is labeled '20 (cap)' and is exactly at the 20 ip Arms cap (correct). The shinden mislabel is cosmetic but could confuse tuning (implies the cap binds when it does not).
findings.suggestedFix: Remove the '(cap)' annotation from the shinden 30 entry (it is under the 32 cap), or raise it to 32 if the intent is for it to sit at the cap. Keep the Arms defend '20 (cap)' label (it is correct).

findings.location: docs/prd-sections/section-4-balance.md §4.5.3 milestone L10 (line 387) vs no-cross-feed guard (lines 392-393)
findings.issue: L10 milestone perk is described as 'a title + +1 related attribute or a recipe unlock'. If a LABOUR skill's L10 grants '+1 related attribute' to STR/AGI (which feed combat via §4.6.1 attackPower/evasion), that is a labour→combat edge through the shared attribute — in tension with the explicit 'no labour-skill→combat-attribute edge exists' guard two lines later. The guard sentence resolves the intent, but the '+1 related attribute' wording is underspecified and could be implemented as a leak.
findings.suggestedFix: Constrain the L10 '+1 related attribute' perk to combat skills only (or to a labour-relevant attribute path that does not feed combat-derived stats), and state explicitly that labour-skill milestones never grant attribute points that affect combat — making the no-cross-feed wall enforceable at the type level as §4.5.3 already claims.

canon.summary: §3 (unlock ladder) and §4 (balance) are broadly canon-compliant with the human-signed §I-bal balance locks and sections D/E/G/H/I. All hard locks are honoured: first-fight 20–35% win-rate (LOCKED-tagged), soft-setback shape exactly as proposed (1 HP + ~½-day + light injury + possible carried-loot drop, never levels/gear/Influence), more-from-deeds accrual (~70/30, LOCKED-tagged), smaller/steadier deed jumps (per-event cap 0.08→0.04), no respec in v1 (§4.4/§4.5.3/§4.9), nav reveal one-tab-at-a-time (§3.5), combat earns Arms but trash mobs don't balloon it (§4.6.5 "only for recognized clears/defends, not every trash mob"), active-only/auto-producers T3+ only, no people-management sim, grounded/no-magic with belief-creatures kept out of spawn tables (Hanzaki as INVESTIGATE-then-confront one-shot; kappa as optional rumour), K/M/B formatting, simple per-tier thresholds with NO floor/overflow (§4.1), and deferred items kept deferred (Standing & Office kanji → §5; marriage-lever numbers → T3). The recent human-approved edits are correctly applied in §3 (Osen rename, T2-G2 dual gate STORY+PILLAR, §4 lock-vs-proposed tagging, Jinpachi/Otsuru names). Three issues found: (1) the §4.8.1 T0 pacing table's R4/R5/R6 reveal annotations are STALE relative to the approved §3 de-densification — it still lists "simple Crafting" and "first Arms standing" at R5 instead of crafting@R4 and the first Arms-standing RECORD@R6; (2) R0 is paced at ~5 min, carved out of the LOCKED ≥30-min-per-rank floor; (3) T0 is set to 4.5h vs the canon's stated "T0 ≈ 4h". None are blockers; (2) and (3) are defensible and prompt-endorsed reconciliations, and (1) is a doc-sync fix.

findings.location: section-4-balance.md §4.8.1 T0 pacing table, lines 618–620 (R4/R5/R6 rows)
findings.issue: The §4.8.1 pacing table's rung reveal annotations are stale relative to the human-approved §3 de-densification (canon §I-bal '... confirms §3.5' context; prompt-stated approved edit (c)). §4 line 619 lists R5 as 'Quest log + 4 quest types, simple Crafting, first Arms standing' — but the approved edit moved simple Crafting + the loot→craft loop UP to R4, and moved the first combat-earned Arms-standing RECORD DOWN to R6. §3 line 91 correctly places the simple Crafting tab at R4; §3 line 92 states R5 only begins Arms-deed accrual and 'the standing itself is first recorded at R6'; §3 line 93 places 'THE FIRST COMBAT-EARNED HOUSE STANDING is recorded' at R6. §4's R4 row (line 618) omits crafting, R5 row (line 619) wrongly attributes crafting and the 'first Arms standing' (record) to R5, and R6 row (line 620) omits the Arms-standing-record reveal. This is a §3↔§4 contradiction on an already-human-signed-off placement.
findings.suggestedFix: Resync the §4.8.1 R4/R5/R6 reveal column to §3: R4 = '...domestic economy, first shinden (E1), simple Crafting + loot→craft loop'; R5 = 'Quest log + 4 quest types; Arms deeds BEGIN accruing' (drop 'simple Crafting' and drop the 'first Arms standing' record label); R6 = 'Workshops/Granary (E2), proto-industry, FIRST combat-earned Arms standing RECORDED, village tier seed'. The deed values (first DEFEND deed, Arms accrual) can stay; only the reveal labels need to match the approved §3 grain.

findings.location: section-4-balance.md §4.8.1, line 614 (R0 row) and line 605/623 (the 'R0 aside' carve-out)
findings.issue: Canon §I-bal LOCKS: 'Per-rank time FLOOR ≈ 30 min — no single rank may be advanced in under ~30 minutes. With 8 rungs, this makes T0 ≥ 4h by construction.' §4.8.1 prices R0 ('Stray') at ~5 min and explicitly carves it out ('Each rung's expected wall-clock ≥ ~30 min (R0 aside, the cold-open story rung)'). R0 is a genuine ladder rung (R0–R7), so a literal reading of the LOCKED clause is violated, and the canon's own '8 rungs × 30 min = T0 ≥ 4h by construction' arithmetic assumes all 8 rungs honour the floor. The design rationale (R0 is the hand-authored cold-open pre-ladder state per §3.1) is reasonable, but it touches a human-LOCKED constraint.
findings.suggestedFix: Either (a) get the human to explicitly bless the R0-as-tutorial-rung carve-out and annotate the lock language to permit it (preferred, since R0 is a story-only cold-open beat and the 4.5h T0 budget is over-satisfied without it), or (b) fold R0 into the cold open / R1 so the ladder's first *advanceable* rung is R1 and the '8 rungs ≥30 min' arithmetic holds literally. Add a one-line note in §4.8.1 citing the human sign-off for the carve-out.

findings.issue: Canon §I-bal states 'T0 ≈ 4h' as the per-tier figure (and 'v1 total ≈ 32h'). §4 uses T0 ≈ 4.5h (and v1 ≈ 28.5h, with the 32h reconciled as including the T3-stub runway/free-play tail). 4.5h is ≥ the canon's '8 rungs × 30 min = T0 ≥ 4h by construction' floor and is consistent with the 'LONGER saga' intent, and the task prompt explicitly endorses '~28.5 h built', so this is a sanctioned reconciliation rather than a violation — but the T0 4h→4.5h delta and the 32h→28.5h reframing should be traceable.
findings.suggestedFix: Keep the numbers, but add an explicit cross-reference note (already partially present in §4's REBALANCE NOTE and §4.8) confirming the human signed off T0 = 4.5h (above the 4h floor) and that '≈32h' = 28.5h core + T3-stub/free-play tail, so a future reader doesn't read it as drift from the literal canon figures.

consistency.summary: §3 (unlock ladder) is internally consistent and correctly reflects the human-approved R5 de-densification: simple Crafting + loot→craft loop at R4 (§3.2 R4 row), Quest log + 4 quest types with Arms deeds only BEGINNING at R5 (§3.2 R5), first combat-earned Arms standing RECORDED at R6 (§3.2 R6), four-bar panel at R7. §3's checklist (§3.9 items 3 & 4) and the §3 header both describe the new layout. All section cross-references (§2.x, §3.x, §5.x, §6.x, §5 T0.x/T1.x/T2.x beats) resolve to real headers. Pillar names (Arms / Estate & Wealth / Standing & Office / Name & Honour) and tier/rank vocab (T0–T4, R/V/G/C/E rungs) are used consistently. The old "~45–75 min / ~12–20 h" pacing targets appear ONLY as explicitly superseded/deleted notes in §4 (lines 24, 600) — never as live targets — and prd.md carries no live stale numeric pacing target. HOWEVER, the §4.8.1 T0 pacing table did NOT receive the R5 de-densification: its R5 row still lists "simple Crafting" (belongs at R4) and "first Arms standing" (the RECORD belongs at R6 per §3) — the central §3↔§4 rung-alignment mismatch. The same stale pre-de-densification layout (crafting + first Arms standing both at R5) persists in §1.5.1, §1.12, §2.10, §2.15, and §5 T0.2 — all referenced by §3's canon-check notes. Two minor terminology issues: a "Toge-mura" macron slip in prd.md §1.7.1, and an E0–E7 (T4 Edo rung) vs E0–E5 (estate-stage) label collision.

findings.issue: The §4.8.1 T0 pacing table (the §4 centrepiece) was NOT updated for the R5 de-densification and now contradicts §3.2. The R5 row label reads 'Quest log + 4 quest types, simple Crafting, **first Arms standing**'. But §3.2 places simple Crafting at R4 (R4 row: 'the simple Crafting tab ... loot→craft loop') and places the first combat-earned Arms-standing RECORD at R6 (R5 only BEGINS accruing Arms; R6 'THE FIRST COMBAT-EARNED HOUSE STANDING is recorded'). So §4.8.1 R5 is wrong on both counts. Correspondingly the §4.8.1 R4 row (line 618) omits 'simple Crafting' and the R6 row (line 620) omits the first-Arms-standing-record reveal.
findings.suggestedFix: Re-align the §4.8.1 R4/R5/R6 rows to §3.2: add 'simple Crafting + loot→craft loop' to the R4 row label; change the R5 row label to 'Quest log + 4 quest types (Arms deeds BEGIN accruing)' and drop 'simple Crafting' and 'first Arms standing' from it; add 'first combat-earned Arms standing RECORDED' to the R6 row label.

findings.location: docs/prd.md §1.5.1 T0 ladder table — R5 row (line 223) and R4 row (line 222)
findings.issue: The §1 locked T0 ladder still carries the OLD pre-de-densification layout that §3 explicitly supersedes. R5 (line 223) lists 'loot + craft (Smith Gonta spearheads via the component chain); **the FIRST combat-earned standing.**' — i.e. crafting AND first Arms standing at R5. §3 moved crafting up to R4 and the first Arms-standing record down to R6. R4 (line 222) correspondingly omits any crafting reveal. §3 (line 91, 93) and §3.9 item 3 cite the new layout, so §1.5.1 now disagrees with the section it feeds. Although §1 is marked 'Locked', this is a leftover the de-densification edit should have propagated.
findings.suggestedFix: Update §1.5.1: move the simple-craft / loot→craft reveal from R5 to R4; in R5 replace 'the FIRST combat-earned standing' with 'PEST-CONTROL/HUNT/CLEAR/DEFEND quest types; Arms deeds begin accruing'; add the first combat-earned Arms-standing record to R6. (Or add an explicit 'superseded by §3' note if §1 must stay frozen.)

findings.issue: Three more §1/§2 cross-reference targets carry the stale R5 layout that §3 supersedes: §1.12 line 681 'R5 — pest-control/hunt/clear/defend quests + craft chain + the first combat-earned standing'; §2.10 line 1178 'The loot + craft loop ... comes online at R5 (gate-guard)'; §2.15 line 1110 'Combat-earned standing first appears at R5 (gate-guard)'. Per the de-densification, the loot→craft loop comes online at R4 and the first combat-earned standing is recorded at R6.
findings.suggestedFix: §1.12 R5 line: drop 'craft chain' (move to R4) and 'first combat-earned standing' (move to R6). §2.10(e): change 'comes online at R5' to 'R4'. §2.15(e): change 'first appears at R5 (gate-guard)' to 'begins accruing at R5 (gate-guard); first recorded at R6 (foreman)'.

findings.issue: §5 T0.2 main-quest beat 7 ('The first standing watch') states 'This produces the **first combat-earned Arms standing** — "the gate held, and the books say so"' at the gate-watch beat, which §3 maps to R5 (Gate-guard). But §3 moved the first Arms-standing RECORD down to R6; at R5 Arms deeds only begin accruing. §3.1's canon-check note and §3.2 reference §5 T0.2 as the authority for the beats, so this is a direct §3↔§5 inconsistency on where the first Arms standing is recorded.
findings.suggestedFix: Reword §5 T0.2 beat 7 so the gate-watch BEGINS earning Arms deeds, and add/adjust a later beat (the foreman-of-works / R6 step) where the first Arms standing is formally recorded ('the books say so'), matching §3.2's earn(R5)→recorded(R6)→seen(R7) staging.

findings.issue: Macron-romanization slip: §1.7.1 writes 'Hibara + Toge-mura' (no macron), whereas §3 (line 201), §4 (line 664), and §5 (prd.md lines 2042, 2153, 2189) all correctly write 'Tōge-mura'. Canon mandates proper-Hepburn macrons project-wide, and §3 cites §1.7.1 for this very hard-cap.
findings.suggestedFix: Change 'Toge-mura' to 'Tōge-mura' in prd.md §1.7.1 line 443.

findings.issue: Rank/stage vocabulary collision: the T4 Edo ladder rung labels are E0–E7 (§3.7.2 'E0–E2', 'E3–E5', 'E7'), which reuse the exact 'E#' namespace already used for estate physical stages E0–E5 (E0 Foreclosure's Edge → E5, used throughout §3.2/§3.3 and §4.7.5). Within §3.7.2 the 'E0–E2'/'E3–E5' rung ranges are textually indistinguishable from estate-stage labels. T4 is roadmap-only, but the overlap is a latent ambiguity in the otherwise-clean R/V/G/C/E rung scheme.
findings.suggestedFix: Give the T4 ladder a distinct rung prefix (e.g. 'D0–D7' or 'Ed0–Ed7') or annotate §3.7.2 to disambiguate Edo rungs from estate stages; mirror in §3.7.1's bridge note.

findings.issue: Side-faction speedup magnitude disagreement: §1.12 (and §1.5.4 line 317) states village+origin multipliers '≈ halve time-to-tier', but §4.9 open-question 6 explicitly notes the old '≈halves' target is 'likely too strong now that tiers are 4–16 h' and proposes ~10–20%. §4 flags this as still-open rather than asserting a conflicting locked value, so it is not a hard contradiction, but the '≈halve' figure in §1 is now a live target §4 is walking back.
findings.suggestedFix: Once the human resolves §4.9 Q6, update §1.12/§1.5.4 from '≈ halve' to the agreed shave (or soften §1 to 'a felt, non-required speedup, magnitude per §4').

s7.summary:
The §7 roadmap draft (/Users/raynos/projects/games/incremental-game-1/docs/prd-sections/section-7-roadmap.md) is verify-clean against canon and the drafted PRD §§1-6. All five verification dimensions pass.

(1) v1 SCOPE matches canon §I + §4: T0-T2 complete + T3 stub + T4 roadmap; the lean cut-set (8-rung ladders x3 = 24 rungs, ~5 grounded mobs, 4 quest types, ~6-8 nodes/tier, the locked skill set, E0->E2) reproduces §I and §1.7 exactly; the PARKED/CUT table (E3-E5, Matagi/Pilgrimage/Scholars network, auto-producers, marriage lever, deep upper-tier nodes, national mitate, no-respec) matches §1.7.1 + §4.7.4 + I-bal. The pacing target (28.5h = T0 4.5h/T1 8h/T2 16h, >=30-min floor) matches §4.8 verbatim, including the correct reconciliation of the canon "~32h" figure as the T3-stub/free-play tail.

(2) MILESTONE ROADMAP M0-M7 is a sensible build order: M0 = deterministic spine + cold open + save; M1-M3 = T0 (labour R0-R2 / combat R3 / quests+crafting+pillars R4-R7); M4 = T1; M5 = T2 incl. the G6 payoff; M6 = balance pass + verifier + T3 stub + polish; M7 = deploy. Each is a vertical slice with a verifiable definition-of-done ending verify-green. The rung sequencing precisely tracks the §3 ladder (combat-live-at-R3, R4 crafting+loot, R5 quest-types-only, R6 first Arms record, R7 four-bar panel, G2 double-gate, G6 payoff, the nav-reveal track) and the de-densification edits the prompt flagged as already-applied. The M2a/M2b split at the R3->R4 seam is well-reasoned.

(3) DEPLOYMENT is concrete and backend-free: build:itch = vite build + zip dist/ (matches §6.1); the npm run verify gate is quoted exactly (tsc + eslint + prettier + vitest + verify-content + gen:docs --check); IndexedDB autosave + base64 export/import per §6.8; explicit "no backend, no network calls."

(4) NO CANON VIOLATIONS: active-only (no idle layer; auto-producers T3+ scaffold-only); no management sim (build/recruit = flavour roster cards); no magic/grounded (~5 grounded mobs, no belief-creatures in spawn tables, the "one-eyed god" is a one-shot); pure-core/one-RNG/determinism honoured. The §7.1.3 hard-guardrails block reproduces every machine-checked §6.6 invariant.

(5) INTERNAL CONSISTENCY: all cross-referenced sections (§§1-6 in prd.md; §3/§4 as separate files) exist; specific cited numbers (§4.1 gate thresholds 0.5K/0.8K, 5K/8K/2K, 60K/50K/30K; first-fight 20-35%; soft-setback shape; ~70/30 split) all match their sources; the canon relative link resolves; the milestone-summary table aligns with the milestone bodies. Findings below are minor/advisory only.

findings.issue: §7 uses T0 ≈ 4.5 h, while the raw human-signed canon §I-bal text says "T0 ≈ 4h". This is NOT a violation: §4.8 reconciled it (8 rungs × the locked ≥30-min floor makes T0 ≥4h "by construction," and §4's rank table sums to 4.5h), and canon explicitly authorizes §4's rebalance to honour the locked shape. §7 correctly inherits §4's 4.5h and even calls out the 28.5h-vs-32h gap. Worth a one-line note for the human so the 4h→4.5h derivation is not mistaken for drift.
findings.suggestedFix: Optionally add a parenthetical in §7.1.2 noting that canon's "T0 ≈ 4h" is the floor and §4.8 derives the built total at ≈4.5h from the 8-rung × ≥30-min construction (so the two are consistent, not conflicting).

findings.issue: The base: './' relative-base deployment detail is introduced new in §7.3 and is not present in §6.1/§6.9. This is appropriate (§7 is the deployment section and the right home for it) and technically correct for itch.io subpath serving, but it is a fresh concrete commitment rather than a cross-reference, so it is not traceable back to §6.
findings.suggestedFix: No change required. If desired, drop a forward-note in §6.1's build line ("base path set at deploy — see §7.3") so the architecture and deployment sections cross-link the one Vite config detail that crosses the boundary.

findings.location: section-7-roadmap.md (header note, lines 4-6) vs repo layout
findings.issue: The draft references §§1,2,5,6 by number; those live in docs/prd.md (not separate section files like §3/§4/§7). The canon link path (../../brainstorms/2026-06-25-locked-decisions.md) resolves correctly and there are no broken in-doc links, but a reader opening only the prd-sections/ folder will not find §§1,2,5,6 as files. This is a layout artifact, not a §7 defect.
findings.suggestedFix: No change to §7 needed. At PRD integration, ensure the section files are concatenated into / reconciled with prd.md so the §-number references all resolve in the assembled document.
